User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user of the MovaFin application, serving as a tenant for all associated financial data. Authentication is handled externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification. Not for authentication.

name.description: The full name or display name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

AccountType.title: AccountType
AccountType.description: Defines a customizable category or type for financial accounts (e.g., Checking, Savings, Cash). Users can manage their own list of account types.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccountType entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this account type. (Relationship: User 1:N AccountType)

name.description: The name of the account type (e.g., 'Conta Corrente', 'Poupança', 'Carteira').

description.description: An optional, more detailed description of the account type.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account type was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account type was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a user's financial account (e.g., bank account, cash wallet) where transactions are recorded.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this account. (Relationship: User 1:N Account)

accountTypeId.description: Reference to the AccountType that classifies this account. (Relationship: AccountType 1:N Account)

name.description: A user-defined name for the account (e.g., 'Minha Conta Principal', 'Cartão de Crédito').

initialBalance.description: The starting balance of the account when it was first created.

description.description: An optional, more detailed description of the account.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the account is currently active and in use.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account was last updated.

TransactionCategory.title: TransactionCategory
TransactionCategory.description: Defines a customizable category for classifying financial transactions as either income or expense. Users can manage their own categories.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransactionCategory entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this transaction category. (Relationship: User 1:N TransactionCategory)

type.description: The nature of the category, indicating if it's primarily for 'Income' or 'Expense' transactions.

description.description: An optional, more detailed description of the category.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction category was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records a single financial movement, either an income (entrada) or an expense (saída), associated with a specific account and category.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the Account where this transaction occurred. (Relationship: Account 1:N Transaction)

transactionCategoryId.description: Reference to the TransactionCategory that classifies this transaction. (Relationship: TransactionCategory 1:N Transaction)

date.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expense.

description.description: Optional notes or a detailed description of the transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

Attachment.title: Attachment
Attachment.description: Represents a file (e.g., receipt, invoice) attached to a financial transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attachment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this attachment. (Relationship: User 1:N Attachment)

transactionId.description: Reference to the Transaction this attachment belongs to. (Relationship: Transaction 1:N Attachment)

fileName.description: The original name of the attached file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the attached file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

fileUrl.description: The URI or path where the attached file is stored.

description.description: An optional description or context for the attachment.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the attachment was uploaded.

definition.description: Root collection for user profiles. Each document represents a MovaFin user and is identified by their Firebase Authentication UID. Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `userId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of the user.

definition.description: Subcollection for customizable account types owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches `resource.data.userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this account type.

params.name: accountTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the account type.

definition.description: Subcollection for financial accounts owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches `resource.data.userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this account.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the financial account.

definition.description: Subcollection for customizable transaction categories (income/expense) owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches `resource.data.userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this transaction category.

params.name: transactionCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction category.

definition.description: Subcollection for individual financial transactions owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId', 'accountId', and 'transactionCategoryId' fields for authorization independence and queryability.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

definition.description: Nested subcollection for file attachments associated with a specific transaction. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'transactionId' for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches `resource.data.userId` and `transactionId` matches the parent transaction.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent transaction and this attachment.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent transaction this attachment belongs to.

params.name: attachmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the attachment.

definition.description: A top-level collection for storing administrator roles (DBAC). The document ID is the UID of an authenticated user. Existence of a document here grants administrative privileges across the application for data viewing and management. Documents can be empty or contain simple audit fields.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of an administrator user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for MovaFin is designed with a strong emphasis on multi-tenancy, security, and scalability, adhering strictly to the core design principles:

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Every document in a user-specific subcollection (e.g., AccountType, Account, TransactionCategory, Transaction, Attachment) explicitly includes the `userId` field. This denormalization means that security rules can evaluate access requests based solely on the `request.auth.uid` matching the `resource.data.userId` without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. This approach is fundamental for supporting atomic operations (transactions, batches) and greatly simplifies rule debugging by removing complex hierarchical dependencies.

2.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure inherently supports Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) through **Structural Segregation** and **Authorization Independence**. All user-specific data is strictly segregated under the `/users/{userId}` path. For example, a user requesting their accounts will query `/users/{request.auth.uid}/accounts`. The security rules will simply check if `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` (which is also derived from the path `userId`). This ensures that `list` operations are implicitly secure, as a user can only query within their dedicated data path. For administrators, a dedicated `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection provides clear DBAC. An admin's presence in this collection allows them to `list` all `/users` documents and then `read` any subcollection for any user, enabling `list` operations across user data without violating QAPs, as the rule for admins will explicitly grant this broader access based on their role existence.

3.  **Structural Segregation:** All data belonging to a specific user (AccountTypes, Accounts, TransactionCategories, Transactions, and their Attachments) resides within subcollections under `/users/{userId}/...`. This ensures that all documents within a given collection path share a homogeneous security posture: they are owned by the `userId` specified in the path and the document's `userId` field. This prevents mixing public and private data or data with different access requirements in the same collection, making security rules simpler and more explicit.

4.  **Access Modeling:**
    *   **Private Data:** All user financial data is private, secured using path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`).
    *   **Hierarchical Paths:** `User 1:N Entity` relationships (User to AccountType, Account, TransactionCategory, Transaction) are modeled using hierarchical paths like `/users/{userId}/accountTypes/{accountTypeId}`. This is a secure and efficient way to manage user-owned data.
    *   **Nested Hierarchy:** `Transaction 1:N Attachment` is further nested under transactions: `/users/{userId}/transactions/{transactionId}/attachments/{attachmentId}`, while still maintaining `userId` and `transactionId` denormalized within the `Attachment` document for authorization independence.
    *   **Global Roles (DBAC):** The `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection implements role-based access control. An `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` grants elevated permissions.

This architecture provides a robust, secure, and easily debuggable foundation for MovaFin, ensuring that each user's financial data is isolated and protected, while also enabling controlled administrator access.
